- This is a Cloudflare Worker that DOES implement caching:

2. L1661: inside the Voice Over IIFE — `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', loadVoiceOvers)` — loads voice overs.

The videos load metadata eagerly via preload="metadata". This is a notable finding: the lazy video observer exists but createReelCard doesn't use data-src, so lazy loading doesn't actually happen — all videos preload metadata on creation.

Let me double-check: createReelCard sets `'<source src="' + item.video + '" type="video/mp4">'`. ## Core Web Vitals Audit — D:\portfouilo\photographyportfolio

File sizes: `script.js` 57.7 KB / 1535 lines, `index.html` 46.9 KB / 763 lines, `styles.css` 88.1 KB / 3543 lines. No service worker (`sw.js`/`service-worker.js`), no `manifest.json`, no `_headers`/`_redirects`, no `<link rel="preload">`, no inline `<style>` blocks, no `navigator.serviceWorker` registration — confirmed via filesystem + content search.

---

### 1. script.js

toolCalls.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

Unless fonts are referenced via a CSS @import that I didn't find (I searched @import and found none). Let me double check — the @import search returned no @import matches (the matches were all @keyframes, content-visibility, will-change, @font-face... wait actually the results show @keyframes and content-visibility and will-change but NO @import and NO @font-face). So:
  - No @import (good, no blocking CSS imports)
  - No @font-face (no custom fonts defined in CSS)
  - The fonts preconnects may be unused/legacy. Interesting finding.

Actually wait — let me reconsider. Maybe fonts are loaded but I should verify. The preconnect to fonts.googleapis.com is there but no <link rel="stylesheet" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com/...">. This means the preconnects are wasted/orphaned OR the site intends to use system fonts. Let me check what font-family is used. Actually that's a minor detail. The key finding: no font CSS link despite preconnects — orphaned preconnects (minor wasted resource hint) and the styles.css is the only render-blocking stylesheet at 88KB.

- content-visibility: auto used at L763, L1167, L1182 (good — 3 usages, helps performance)
- Heavy use of backdrop-filter (many lines, ~30+ occurrences) and box-shadow (~70+ occurrences) and filter:blur(80px) at L184 for bg-glow. These are expensive, especially backdrop-filter which is GPU-heavy.
- will-change used in several places (good practice but overuse can hurt).
- @keyframes: glow-float (L208), pageEnter (L360), shimmer (L1003), ripple (L1013), fadeIn (L1714), scaleIn (L2193), cv-fade-in (L3139), cv-scale-in (L3217), vo-float (L3516), vo-spin (L3736).
- The resize handler (script.js L1013) removes 'active' then forces reflow via `void el.offsetWidth` then re-adds 'active' — this is layout thrashing on breakpoint cross. Only runs on breakpoint change though, debounced 250ms.

**worker/src/index.js:**
- This is a Cloudflare Worker that DOES implement caching:
  - Edge cache (Cache API): 5 minutes (caches.default, cache.match/cache.put)
  - Browser cache: 5 minutes via Cache-Control header
  - L113: `Cache-Control': public, max-age=${CACHE_TTL}, s-maxage=${CACHE_TTL * 2}`
  - CACHE_TTL likely 300 (5 min), s-maxage 600
- This appears to be a separate worker (maybe the worker serves the /api/videos equivalent or proxies R2). Note the Pages Function videos.js does NOT set cache headers — only this worker does. So there are two backends.

**Service worker:** None found. No sw.js, no service-worker.js. No manifest.json. So no PWA/service worker caching at the browser level (no offline, no runtime caching of video assets). Only wrangler.toml exists (for the worker).

**Caching gap:** The functions/api/videos.js (Pages Function) sets NO Cache-Control headers and does NOT use the Cache API. Only the Cloudflare Worker (worker/src/index.js) does edge caching. Need to verify which one is actually deployed/used. The script.js fetches '/api/videos' which is the Pages Function — so the actually-used endpoint has NO caching! This is a key finding.

Let me verify: script.js fetchAllMedia calls `fetch('/api/videos')` (L~135). That's the Pages Function. The worker is a separate deployment (wrangler.toml in worker/). So the Pages Function (the one actually used) has zero caching.

**index.html head links:**
- L13: `<link rel="canonical" ...>`
- L14: `<link rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css">` — render-blocking, 88KB, NOT preloaded
- L15: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com">`
- L16: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin>`
- L17: `<link rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://pub-...r2.dev">`
- L18: `<link rel="preconnect" href="https://pub-...r2.dev" crossorigin>`
- No `<link rel="preload">` for any resource (no preload of hero image, no preload of fonts, no preload of critical JS)
- The stylesheet is the only render-blocking resource, loaded before preconnects.

Note: stylesheet link is BEFORE the preconnects — ordering means preconnects won't help the stylesheet. Also styles.css is render-blocking and large.

**Script tag:** L762 (near end): `<script src="script.js" defer></script>` — uses defer ✓ (good, non-blocking). It's at the bottom of body.

**Inline blocking CSS:** No `<style>` blocks in head (only JSON-LD scripts which are non-blocking). Good. The only render-blocking CSS is the external styles.css.

**Font loading strategy:** preconnects present but NO actual font stylesheet link and NO @font-face — orphaned preconnects. Site likely uses system fonts. This means the preconnects to fonts.googleapis.com/gstatic.com are wasted (minor).

Now, the DOMContentLoaded listeners — there are TWO:
1. L883: main DOMContentLoaded — loads videos, search, models, media buyer, sets up reveal observers, force-activates, defers enhanceReelCards via requestIdleCallback.
2. L1661: inside the Voice Over IIFE — `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', loadVoiceOvers)` — loads voice overs.

Both will fire. The IIFE runs at parse time (registers the listener). Note: since script.js is `defer`, it executes after DOM parse but before DOMContentLoaded event, so both listeners are registered in time. Good.

Heavy synchronous ops on load:
- DOMContentLoaded handler calls loadVideoCategories() (async, fetches /api/videos), loadModels(), loadMediaBuyer() — these are async fetches, not blocking, but they kick off network.
- Multiple getBoundingClientRect() calls in forceActivateRevealCards (L283-289, L689-695, L956-962) — these cause layout/reflow but are batched in rAF. Force-activating all reveal elements on load iterates querySelectorAll and getBoundingClientRect per element — can cause layout thrashing if many elements.
- The resize handler forces reflow (`void el.offsetWidth`) but only on breakpoint change.

IntersectionObserver configs:
1. lazyVideoObserver (L191-203): rootMargin '300px 0px', threshold 0.01
2. revealObserver (L866-878): rootMargin '0px 0px -100px 0px', threshold 0.1

createReelCard (L177-184): creates <article.reel-card> with innerHTML containing a <video controls ... preload="metadata"> and a <source src=... type="video/mp4">. Note: it sets src directly (not data-src), and preload="metadata". So video metadata loads immediately for each card. BUT — wait, there's lazyVideoObserver that expects `source.dataset.src` and swaps it. However createReelCard sets `source.src` directly (not dataset.src). So the lazyVideoObserver logic (which looks for source.dataset.src) would find nothing to swap — meaning the lazy-loading observer is effectively DEAD CODE / not wired up to createReelCard! The videos load metadata eagerly via preload="metadata". This is a notable finding: the lazy video observer exists but createReelCard doesn't use data-src, so lazy loading doesn't actually happen — all videos preload metadata on creation.

Let me double-check: createReelCard sets `'<source src="' + item.video + '" type="video/mp4">'`. Yes, direct src. lazyVideoObserver checks `source.dataset.src` and if present sets `source.src = source.dataset.src`. Since createReelCard never sets data-src, the observer's swap never fires. So lazyVideoObserver.unobserve is called but no lazy swap. Actually the observer.observe is never even called on reel cards anywhere — let me verify. Searching... renderVideoCategory observes `revealObserver.observe(card)` not lazyVideoObserver. I don't see lazyVideoObserver.observe(...) called anywhere. So lazyVideoObserver is completely unused/dead code. Good finding.

setTimeout/requestIdleCallback/requestAnimationFrame usage — I have the list (23 matches). Key ones:
- L969-972: requestIdleCallback(enhanceReelCards) or setTimeout fallback 200ms — good deferral
- L951: setTimeout 600ms page-transition removal
- L660/665: setTimeout 50ms for scrollIntoView
- L283/689/956: double rAF for force-activate
- L1448/1451: rAF loop for audio waveform progress
- Various setTimeout for video play (1718, 1755, 1760)

Event listener patterns / passive:
- L490: scroll listener WITH { passive: true } ✓
- L1013: resize listener — NO passive option (resize doesn't need passive; passive only matters for touch/wheel/scroll). resize isn't passive-eligible. Fine.
- L1570: voCanvas touchstart { passive: true } ✓
- L1574: voCanvas touchmove { passive: false } — intentionally non-passive to call preventDefault (needed for seeking). Acceptable.
- L1781/1785: viewer touchstart/touchend { passive: true } ✓
- L1624: window resize (vo redraw) — no passive (not needed)
- No touchmove on window/document without passive except the intentional one.

So the passive situation is actually good — scroll is passive, touches are passive except the one intentional preventDefault.

Caching in videos.js (Pages Function): NO Cache-Control headers, NO Cache API. jsonResponse only sets Content-Type + CORS. This is the endpoint actually used by the frontend (fetch('/api/videos')). So no caching on the primary data endpoint — every page load hits R2 and lists all objects. This is a significant performance issue: listAllObjects paginates through R2 (1000 per page) for every folder on every request.

Worker (worker/src/index.js): DOES cache — Cache API 5 min, Cache-Control public max-age=300 s-maxage=600. But this worker may not be the one serving /api/videos (the Pages Function is). Need to flag the discrepancy.

File sizes: `script.js` 57.7 KB / 1535 lines, `index.html` 46.9 KB / 763 lines, `styles.css` 88.1 KB / 3543 lines. No service worker (`sw.js`/`service-worker.js`), no `manifest.json`, no `_headers`/`_redirects`, no `<link rel="preload">`, no inline `<style>` blocks, no `navigator.serviceWorker` registration — confirmed via filesystem + content search.

---

### 1. script.js

**DOMContentLoaded listeners — there are TWO (both fire):**
- **L883** (main handler): `window.scrollTo(0,0)`; calls `loadVideoCategories()` (fetches `/api/videos`), `initVideoSearch()`, `loadModels()`, `loadMediaBuyer()`; queries all `.reveal*` and observes them; adds reveal classes to profile/portfolio/footer/avatar/bio; toggles `page-transition` (setTimeout 600ms, L951); force-activates in-viewport reveals via double `requestAnimationFrame` (L956–962); defers `enhanceReelCards()` via `requestIdleCallback` (L969) with `setTimeout(200)` fallback (L972).
- **L1661** (inside the Voice Over IIFE): `document.addEventListener('DOMContentLoaded', loadVoiceOvers)` — fetches `data/voiceover.json` and builds cards.
- Because the script is `defer`-loaded, both listeners register in time. No conflict, but two separate init paths.

**Heavy synchronous / layout operations on load:**
- `forceActivateRevealCards` (L281–292) and its inline duplicates (L688–696, L956–962) iterate `querySelectorAll('.reveal:not(.active)...')` and call `getBoundingClientRect()` per element inside double-rAF — causes layout/reflow across all reveal elements on every page nav + initial load.
- The resize handler (L1013–1024) on breakpoint cross does `el.classList.remove('active'); void el.offsetWidth; el.classList.add('active')` — forced reflow per element (only on mobile↔desktop change, debounced 250ms).
- `pauseAllVideos()` (L57) queries all `<video>` and pauses — cheap but runs on every SPA nav.
- DOMContentLoaded kicks off 4 parallel fetches (videos API + models + media-buyer JSON + later voiceover) — network-heavy but async/non-blocking.

**IntersectionObserver configurations (2 observers):**
- `lazyVideoObserver` (L191–203): `rootMargin: '300px 0px'`, `threshold: 0.01`.
- `revealObserver` (L860–878): `root: null`, `rootMargin: '0px 0px -100px 0px'`, `threshold: 0.1`.

**createReelCard — L177–184** builds `<article class="reel-card">` with `innerHTML` = a `<video controls controlsList="nodownload noplaybackrate" disablePictureInPicture playsinline preload="metadata">` containing `<source src="..." type="video/mp4">`.
- ⚠️ **Key finding:** `createReelCard` sets `source.src` directly and uses `preload="metadata"`, so every rendered video starts loading metadata immediately. The `lazyVideoObserver` (L191) swaps `source.dataset.src` → `source.src`, but `createReelCard` never sets `data-src`, and **`lazyVideoObserver.observe()` is never called anywhere**. The lazy-video observer is effectively dead code — videos are NOT lazily loaded; all preload metadata on render (could be dozens of videos on category pages).

**setTimeout / requestIdleCallback / requestAnimationFrame usage (23 matches):** throttle reset (L40), debounce (L53), 5s iOS loading fallback (L73), double-rAF force-activate (L283/689/956), ripple cleanup 600ms (L546), 50ms scrollIntoView (L660/665), page-transition 600ms (L951), `requestIdleCallback(enhanceReelCards)`/fallback (L969–972), vo focus 100ms (L1227), rAF audio progress loop (L1448/1451), vo resize 250ms (L1626), cinematic play delays 200–300ms (L1718/1755/1760).

**Event-listener / passive audit (71 matches):**
- ✅ `window 'scroll'` (L490) IS passive: `throttle(updateActiveNavLink,100), { passive: true }`.
- ✅ `voCanvas 'touchstart'` (L1570) `{ passive: true }`; viewer `touchstart`/`touchend` (L1781/1785) `{ passive: true }`.
- ⚠️ `voCanvas 'touchmove'` (L1574) is `{ passive: false }` — intentional, calls `e.preventDefault()` during seek. Acceptable but worth noting.
- `window 'resize'` listeners (L1013 debounced 250ms; L1624 vo redraw 250ms) — no passive flag (not required for resize).
- No bare `scroll`/`touchmove` on window/document missing passive. Passive discipline is good overall.
- Several `document.addEventListener('click', …, true)` capture-phase listeners (L1632 nav-away audio stop, L1807 viewer close) — fine.

---

### 2. index.html (head & script)

Head `<link>` tags (L13–18):
- L13 `rel="canonical"`
- L14 `rel="stylesheet" href="styles.css"` — **the only stylesheet, render-blocking, 88 KB, not preloaded**. Placed before the preconnects.
- L15 `rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.googleapis.com"`
- L16 `rel="preconnect" href="https://fonts.gstatic.com" crossorigin`
- L17 `rel="dns-prefetch" href="https://pub-…r2.dev"`
- L18 `rel="preconnect" href="https://pub-…r2.dev" crossorigin`
- ⚠️ **No `<link rel="preload">`** for the hero/cover image, fonts, or critical JS.
- ⚠️ **Orphaned font preconnects:** there is no Google Fonts stylesheet `<link>` and **no `@font-face` in styles.css** (search confirmed) — the `fonts.googleapis.com`/`fonts.gstatic.com` preconnects appear unused (site uses system/emoji fonts). Minor wasted resource hints.

Script tag: **L762 `<script src="script.js" defer></script>`** — ✅ uses `defer`, at end of `<body>`, non-blocking. No `async` scripts.

Inline blocking CSS: **none** — head contains only JSON-LD `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks (non-executing, non-blocking). Good.

Font loading strategy: preconnects only; no font CSS link, no `@font-face`, no `font-display`. Effectively system fonts → no FOIT/FOUT, but the preconnects are wasted.

---

### 3. styles.css (88.1 KB, 3543 lines — large render-blocking sheet)

- **No `@import`** statements (good — no blocking CSS imports).
- **`content-visibility: auto` used 3×** (L763, L1167, L1182) — ✅ good for render perf on off-screen sections.
- **`will-change`** declared ~6× (L310/316/322/327/336/342/988) — used with reset to `auto` (good practice).
- ⚠️ **Expensive properties — heavy usage:** ~30+ `backdrop-filter` (with `-webkit-` duplicates) e.g. L595/635/729/824/894/1059/1535/1629/1705/1749/1789/1997/2158/2187/2539/3148/3167/3191/3422/3553; `filter: blur(80px)` on `bg-glow` (L184) + `filter: brightness/drop-shadow` (L461/485/2085); ~70+ `box-shadow` declarations. `backdrop-filter` + large blurs are GPU/paint-expensive and hurt on low-end mobile (INP/LCP risk).
- **`@keyframes` animations (10):** glow-float (L208), pageEnter (L360), shimmer (L1003), ripple (L1013), fadeIn (L1714), scaleIn (L2193), cv-fade-in (L3139), cv-scale-in (L3217), vo-float (L3516), vo-spin (L3736) — mostly opacity/transform (GPU-friendly), but `shimmer` animates `background-position` (L988 `will-change: background-position`) which can cause paint on the loading skeleton.
- ⚠️ No `@font-face` (confirmed) — consistent with orphaned preconnects above.

---

### 4. functions/api/videos.js (Pages Function — the endpoint the frontend actually uses)

- script.js `fetchAllMedia()` fetches **`/api/videos`** → this Pages Function.
- **Caching: NONE.** `jsonResponse()` (L10–18) sets only `Content-Type` + CORS headers. No `Cache-Control`, no `Cache-API` (`caches`), no `s-maxage`. Every request paginates R2 via `listAllObjects` (1000/page, all folders) — `listAllObjects` L48–62, folder loop L90–110. ⚠️ This is the biggest backend perf gap: the live data endpoint is uncached and re-scans the entire R2 bucket on every page load.
- Has an in-memory `mediaCache` (5-min TTL) only on the **client** side (script.js L134–137), which helps repeat navigations but not cold loads.

---

### 5. worker/src/index.js (separate Cloudflare Worker — NOT used by the frontend)

- This worker serves **`/api/media`** (not `/api/videos`): `GET /`, `GET /api/media`, `GET /api/media?category=` (L6–9). The frontend fetches `/api/videos`, so **this cached worker is not the one being called** — a deployment/route mismatch.
- **It DOES cache properly:** `const CACHE_TTL = 300` (L51); edge Cache API via `caches.default` + `cache.match`/`cache.put` (L81–86, L117–118); `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=600` (L113). 5-min edge + 5-min browser. This is the model to replicate in the Pages Function.

### Service worker / cache config
- **No service worker, no web manifest, no `_headers`/`_redirects`.** Only `worker/wrangler.toml` exists. No client-side runtime caching of HTML/CSS/JS/video assets; the browser relies on default HTTP caching only.

---

### Top optimization opportunities (ranked)
1. **Add caching to `functions/api/videos.js`** (the live endpoint): add `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=600` + use the Cache API (mirror worker/src/index.js L81–118). Currently every load re-scans R2.
2. **Fix the lazy-video dead code:** `createReelCard` (L177) sets `src` + `preload="metadata"` directly and `lazyVideoObserver.observe()` is never called — all videos load metadata eagerly. Either wire up `data-src` + observe, or set `preload="none"` and swap on intersection.
3. **Reduce/condition `backdrop-filter` + `filter: blur(80px)`** in styles.css (L184, ~30 sites) — paint cost on mobile.
4. **Preload critical assets** in `<head>` (LCP image / cover, the `styles.css` itself, or self-host fonts); remove orphaned `fonts.googleapis.com`/`fonts.gstatic.com` preconnects (L15–16) since no font CSS/`@font-face` exists.
5. **Batch the `getBoundingClientRect` force-activate loops** (L283/689/956) to reduce layout thrash on load/nav.
6. Consider registering a service worker for asset/video caching (none exists today).
args.next_steps: Implement the ranked fixes above, starting with #1 (add Cache-Control + Cache API to functions/api/videos.js, copying the pattern from worker/src/index.js L81–118) and #2 (wire up lazy video loading in createReelCard/script.js L177/L191, or set preload="none"). Verify whether worker/src/index.js (/api/media) is a stale/alternate deployment that should replace the Pages Function, or be removed.

Script tag: **L762 `<script src="script.js" defer></script>`** — ✅ uses `defer`, at end of `<body>`, non-blocking. No `async` scripts.

Inline blocking CSS: **none** — head contains only JSON-LD `<script type="application/ld+json">` blocks (non-executing, non-blocking). Good.

Font loading strategy: preconnects only; no font CSS link, no `@font-face`, no `font-display`. Effectively system fonts → no FOIT/FOUT, but the preconnects are wasted.

---

### 3. styles.css (88.1 KB, 3543 lines — large render-blocking sheet)

- **No `@import`** statements (good — no blocking CSS imports).
- **`content-visibility: auto` used 3×** (L763, L1167, L1182) — ✅ good for render perf on off-screen sections.
- **`will-change`** declared ~6× (L310/316/322/327/336/342/988) — used with reset to `auto` (good practice).
- ⚠️ **Expensive properties — heavy usage:** ~30+ `backdrop-filter` (with `-webkit-` duplicates) e.g. L595/635/729/824/894/1059/1535/1629/1705/1749/1789/1997/2158/2187/2539/3148/3167/3191/3422/3553; `filter: blur(80px)` on `bg-glow` (L184) + `filter: brightness/drop-shadow` (L461/485/2085); ~70+ `box-shadow` declarations. `backdrop-filter` + large blurs are GPU/paint-expensive and hurt on low-end mobile (INP/LCP risk).
- **`@keyframes` animations (10):** glow-float (L208), pageEnter (L360), shimmer (L1003), ripple (L1013), fadeIn (L1714), scaleIn (L2193), cv-fade-in (L3139), cv-scale-in (L3217), vo-float (L3516), vo-spin (L3736) — mostly opacity/transform (GPU-friendly), but `shimmer` animates `background-position` (L988 `will-change: background-position`) which can cause paint on the loading skeleton.
- ⚠️ No `@font-face` (confirmed) — consistent with orphaned preconnects above.

---

### 4. functions/api/videos.js (Pages Function — the endpoint the frontend actually uses)

- script.js `fetchAllMedia()` fetches **`/api/videos`** → this Pages Function.
- **Caching: NONE.** `jsonResponse()` (L10–18) sets only `Content-Type` + CORS headers. No `Cache-Control`, no `Cache-API` (`caches`), no `s-maxage`. Every request paginates R2 via `listAllObjects` (1000/page, all folders) — `listAllObjects` L48–62, folder loop L90–110. ⚠️ This is the biggest backend perf gap: the live data endpoint is uncached and re-scans the entire R2 bucket on every page load.
- Has an in-memory `mediaCache` (5-min TTL) only on the **client** side (script.js L134–137), which helps repeat navigations but not cold loads.

---

### 5. worker/src/index.js (separate Cloudflare Worker — NOT used by the frontend)

- This worker serves **`/api/media`** (not `/api/videos`): `GET /`, `GET /api/media`, `GET /api/media?category=` (L6–9). The frontend fetches `/api/videos`, so **this cached worker is not the one being called** — a deployment/route mismatch.
- **It DOES cache properly:** `const CACHE_TTL = 300` (L51); edge Cache API via `caches.default` + `cache.match`/`cache.put` (L81–86, L117–118); `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=600` (L113). 5-min edge + 5-min browser. This is the model to replicate in the Pages Function.

### Service worker / cache config
- **No service worker, no web manifest, no `_headers`/`_redirects`.** Only `worker/wrangler.toml` exists. No client-side runtime caching of HTML/CSS/JS/video assets; the browser relies on default HTTP caching only.

---

### Top optimization opportunities (ranked)
1. **Add caching to `functions/api/videos.js`** (the live endpoint): add `Cache-Control: public, max-age=300, s-maxage=600` + use the Cache API (mirror worker/src/index.js L81–118). Currently every load re-scans R2.
2. **Fix the lazy-video dead code:** `createReelCard` (L177) sets `src` + `preload="metadata"` directly and `lazyVideoObserver.observe()` is never called — all videos load metadata eagerly. Either wire up `data-src` + observe, or set `preload="none"` and swap on intersection.
3. **Reduce/condition `backdrop-filter` + `filter: blur(80px)`** in styles.css (L184, ~30 sites) — paint cost on mobile.
4. **Preload critical assets** in `<head>` (LCP image / cover, the `styles.css` itself, or self-host fonts); remove orphaned `fonts.googleapis.com`/`fonts.gstatic.com` preconnects (L15–16) since no font CSS/`@font-face` exists.
5. **Batch the `getBoundingClientRect` force-activate loops** (L283/689/956) to reduce layout thrash on load/nav.
6. Consider registering a service worker for asset/video caching (none exists today).

Next steps:
Implement the ranked fixes above, starting with #1 (add Cache-Control + Cache API to functions/api/videos.js, copying the pattern from worker/src/index.js L81–118) and #2 (wire up lazy video loading in createReelCard/script.js L177/L191, or set preload="none"). Verify whether worker/src/index.js (/api/media) is a stale/alternate deployment that should replace the Pages Function, or be removed.

functionDeclarations.name: glob
functionDeclarations.description: Efficiently finds files matching specific glob patterns (e.g., `src/**/*.ts`, `**/*.md`), returning absolute paths sorted by modification time (newest first). Ideal for quickly locating files based on their name or path structure, especially in large codebases.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match against (e.g., '**/*.py', 'docs/*.md').

path.description: Optional: The absolute path to the directory to search within. If omitted, searches the root directory.

case_sensitive.description: Optional: Whether the search should be case-sensitive. Defaults to false.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror search content tools. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of file paths to return (default: 100). Use to limit output for broad patterns. When the cap is hit the result is paginated; pass 'offset' set to the previous offset+maxResults to continue.

offset.description: Optional: Number of matching file paths to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

functionDeclarations.name: list_directory
functionDeclarations.description: Lists the names of files and subdirectories directly within a specified directory path. Can optionally ignore entries matching provided glob patterns.

path.description: The absolute path to the directory to list (must be absolute, not relative)

ignore.description: List of glob patterns to ignore

functionDeclarations.name: read_file
functionDeclarations.description: Reads and returns the content of a specified file. By default reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. Files whose selected range exceeds 256 KB are truncated with an advisory; use 'offset' and 'limit' only if the file is too large to read at once — it's recommended to read the whole file by not providing these parameters. Handles text files. For images, PDFs, videos, audio or other multimedia files, this tool cannot present their contents to the model — use the 'analyze_multimedia' tool instead.

absolute_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read (e.g., '/home/user/project/file.txt'). Relative paths are not supported. You must provide an absolute path.

offset.description: Optional: For text files, the 0-based line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once. Note: search_file_content reports matches as 1-based `Lxx` line numbers; to jump from a hit at `L42` here, subtract 1 — pass `offset: 41` (i.e. `offset = lineNumber - 1`).

limit.description: Optional: For text files, the maximum number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

functionDeclarations.name: run_shell_command
functionDeclarations.description:
This tool executes a given shell command using the best available Windows shell (PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt).

IMPORTANT:
- This tool is for terminal operations (e.g., git / npm / docker / build & test commands).
- DO NOT use this tool for file operations like reading, writing, editing, or searching files. Prefer the dedicated file tools for those actions.
- Quote any file path that contains spaces with double quotes, e.g. `cd "/path with spaces"` or `python "/path with spaces/script.py"`.
- If a command would produce very large output, prefer adding flags/filters (narrow paths, limit counts, request specific fields, machine-readable formats like JSON) to keep output small and unambiguous.
- Very large outputs may be returned as the most recent tail of the output. Earlier content may be truncated from the beginning. If the tool reports a "Full output saved to: <path>", that file contains the full command output. Use the exact path returned by the tool, then prefer searching that file first (grep/rg or platform equivalents) for specific ERROR/WARN/FAIL/SUCCESS markers, then reading small slices (with `read_file` using `offset`/`limit`) or tailing the log file instead of re-running expensive commands.
- Do not append truncating pipelines such as `| tail`, `| Select-Object -Last`, or similar to an expensive first run just to suppress output. Those discard earlier context and force a re-run if you later need omitted lines. Let the tool/log capture the full output, then inspect the saved log file.
- When issuing multiple shell commands in one response, parallelize only when they are truly independent and do not compete for the same files, directories, logs, locks, caches, git state, or ports. If one command depends on another or shared state may race, run them sequentially.

The following information is returned:
- Command: Executed command.
- Directory: Directory where command was executed, or `(root)`.
- Output: Combined command output (stdout/stderr). Can be `(empty)` or an excerpt if very large.
- Error: Error or `(none)` if no error was reported for the subprocess.
- Exit Code: Exit code or `(none)` if terminated by signal.
- Signal: Signal number or `(none)` if no signal was received.

command.description: Exact command to execute using the best available Windows shell (automatically selected from PowerShell Core, Windows PowerShell, or Command Prompt). Quote any file paths containing spaces with double quotes.

description.description: Brief description of the command for the user. Be specific and concise. Ideally a single sentence. Can be up to 3 sentences for clarity. No line breaks.

directory.description: (OPTIONAL) The absolute path of the directory to run the command in. If not provided, the project root directory is used. Must be a directory within the workspace and must already exist.

functionDeclarations.name: search_file_content
functionDeclarations.description: Searches for a regular expression pattern within the content of files in a specified directory, a specific file, or the current working directory. The search backend is ripgrep, so regex patterns use ripgrep/Rust regex syntax, not JavaScript RegExp syntax. If you need to search literal text that contains regex metacharacters (for example $, {, [, (, or \), set fixed_strings: true or -F: true instead of escaping by hand. Can filter files by a glob pattern when searching directories. By default, returns matching lines with file paths and line numbers; set output_mode to "files_with_matches" for just file paths or "count" for per-file match totals. Each matched line is truncated at 500 characters with a "[line truncated from N chars]" suffix to keep minified or base64 content from blowing up context.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for within file contents. This is interpreted by ripgrep/Rust regex, not JavaScript RegExp; avoid JS-only features such as lookbehind and backreferences. Regex syntax errors are returned by ripgrep. For literal text, especially strings containing regex metacharacters like $, {, [, (, or \, prefer fixed_strings: true or -F: true. When passing JSON args, escape only for JSON once: to match literal ${, use pattern "\\$\\{" (the regex engine sees \$\{), not "\\\\$\\\\{".

path.description: Optional: A path relative to the workspace root, or an absolute path to a directory or file to search within. If a file path is provided, searches only within that file. If a directory path is provided, searches all files in that directory. If omitted, searches the current working directory.

glob.description: Optional: A glob pattern to filter which files are searched (e.g., '*.js', '*.{ts,tsx}', 'src/**'). Single string only — combine multiple patterns via whitespace/comma separation (e.g. '*.js *.ts', '*.js,*.ts') or brace expansion ('*.{ts,tsx}'). Only applicable when searching directories, ignored for file searches. If omitted, searches all files (respecting potential global ignores).

output_mode.description: Optional: Output format. 'content' (default) returns matching lines with line numbers and optional context; 'files_with_matches' returns only the list of matching file paths; 'count' returns per-file match counts.

maxResults.description: Optional: Maximum number of matches to return to prevent context overflow (default: 250, max: 1000). Values above 1000 are clamped automatically. Use lower values for broad searches, higher for specific searches. Pass 0 for unlimited.

head_limit.description: Optional: Claude Code aligned alias for 'maxResults'. When both are provided, head_limit wins. Pass 0 for unlimited (use sparingly — large result sets waste context).

offset.description: Optional: Number of matches to skip before returning results. Use this to continue after a truncated result's next offset.

-A.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -A.

-B.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -B.

-C.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for ripgrep's -C.

context.description: Optional: Number of context lines to show before and after each match. Only applies to 'content' output_mode. Alias for -C.

-n.description: Optional: Whether to include line numbers in 'content' output. Defaults to true; set to false to omit them.

-i.description: Optional: Case-insensitive matching. Defaults to false (case-sensitive), matching ripgrep behavior.

fixed_strings.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for -F.

-F.description: Optional: Treat the pattern as a literal string instead of a regular expression. Alias for fixed_strings.

type.description: Optional: ripgrep file type filter (rg --type). Common types: js, py, rust, go, java, etc. Accepts any alias from `rg --type-list`; the value is forwarded verbatim to ripgrep.

multiline.description: Optional: Enable multiline matching where patterns may span newlines (passes -U and --multiline-dotall to ripgrep). Only meaningful for 'content' output_mode. ^/$ anchors keep line boundaries behavior for portability across search backends.

include_hidden.description: Optional: Whether to descend into dot-prefixed directories (e.g. '.git/', '.codely-cli/') and match dot-prefixed files. Defaults to false to mirror ripgrep's default hidden-skip behaviour. Set to true to scan hidden paths explicitly (passes --hidden to ripgrep).
